# Finlynq

> Open-source (AGPL v3) personal finance app with a first-party Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. Track income, expenses, budgets, investments, loans, and goals — then query your data in natural language from Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, or any MCP client. Self-host with Docker, or use the free managed cloud at finlynq.com/cloud.

Finlynq is the only open-source, self-hostable personal finance manager (PFM) with a shipped first-party MCP server (91 HTTP / 87 stdio tools). Per-user envelope encryption (AES-256-GCM with a scrypt-derived key) means even the operator cannot read your transaction payees, notes, tags, account names, or category names. It is bootstrapped and donation-funded — no paid tiers, no advertising, no sale of user data. Not affiliated with Finq.com (a forex/CFD broker) or Finlync (a B2B treasury platform).

## Core pages
- [What is Finlynq?](https://finlynq.com/about): Overview, what makes it different, brand disambiguation, and FAQ.
- [MCP guide](https://finlynq.com/mcp-guide): Connect Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, and other MCP clients (OAuth 2.1 + DCR, Bearer API key, or stdio).
- [MCP tool catalog](https://finlynq.com/mcp-guide/tools): The full list of HTTP + stdio MCP tools, grouped by category.
- [Self-hosting](https://finlynq.com/self-hosted): Run Finlynq on your own infrastructure with Docker Compose.
- [Managed cloud](https://finlynq.com/cloud): Free hosted edition, same features as self-host.

## Comparisons
- [Finlynq vs Monarch Money](https://finlynq.com/vs/monarch): Open-source self-host vs polished closed SaaS with mature US bank sync.
- [Finlynq vs Era](https://finlynq.com/vs/era): Two MCP-first PFMs — open-source + self-hostable vs closed hosted SaaS.
- [Finlynq vs Firefly III](https://finlynq.com/vs/firefly-iii): Two open-source self-hostable PFMs — first-party MCP + name encryption vs mature double-entry.
- [Finlynq vs Alderfi](https://finlynq.com/vs/alderfi): Shipped, encrypted, MCP-first Finlynq compared with Alderfi.

## Writing
- [How Finlynq encrypts your money](https://finlynq.com/blog/how-finlynq-encrypts-your-money): Envelope encryption explained in plain English — AES-256-GCM, a scrypt-derived key from your password, a per-user DEK, and the honest tradeoffs.

## Concepts (glossary)
- [What is an MCP server?](https://finlynq.com/glossary/mcp-server)
- [What is envelope encryption?](https://finlynq.com/glossary/envelope-encryption)

- [What is a zero-knowledge personal finance app?](https://finlynq.com/glossary/zero-knowledge-personal-finance)
- [What is lot-tracked cost basis?](https://finlynq.com/glossary/lot-tracked-cost-basis)
